module dm_csrs #(
    parameter int NrHarts = -1
) (
    input  logic                              clk_i,              // Clock
    input  logic                              rst_ni,             // Asynchronous reset active low
    input  logic                              testmode_i,
    input  logic                              dmi_rst_ni,         // Debug Module Interface reset, active-low
    input  logic                              dmi_req_valid_i,
    output logic                              dmi_req_ready_o,
    input  dm::dmi_req_t                      dmi_req_i,
    // every request needs a response one cycle later
    output logic                              dmi_resp_valid_o,
    input  logic                              dmi_resp_ready_i,
    output dm::dmi_resp_t                     dmi_resp_o,
    // global ctrl
    output logic                              ndmreset_o,      // non-debug module reset, active-high
    output logic                              dmactive_o,      // 1 -> debug-module is active, 0 -> synchronous re-set
    // hart status
    input  dm::hartinfo_t [NrHarts-1:0]       hartinfo_i,      // static hartinfo
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                halted_i,        // hart is halted
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                unavailable_i,   // e.g.: powered down
    input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumeack_i,     // hart acknowledged resume request
    // hart control
    output logic [19:0]                       hartsel_o,       // hartselect to ctrl module
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                haltreq_o,       // request to halt a hart
    output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumereq_o,     // request hart to resume

    output logic                              cmd_valid_o,       // debugger is writing to the command field
    output dm::command_t                      cmd_o,             // abstract command
    input  logic                              cmderror_valid_i,  // an error occured
    input  dm::cmderr_t                       cmderror_i,        // this error occured
    input  logic                              cmdbusy_i,         // cmd is currently busy executing

    output logic [dm::ProgBufSize-1:0][31:0]  progbuf_o, // to system bus
    output logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_o,

    input  logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_i,
    input  logic                              data_valid_i,
    // system bus access module (SBA)
    output logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_o,
    input  logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_i,
    output logic                              sbaddress_write_valid_o,
    // control signals in
    output logic                              sbreadonaddr_o,
    output logic                              sbautoincrement_o,
    output logic [2:0]                        sbaccess_o,
    // data out
    output logic                              sbreadondata_o,
    output logic [63:0]                       sbdata_o,
    output logic

606:         // Explains why Debug Mode was entered.
607:         // When there are multiple reasons to enter Debug Mode in a single cycle, hardware should set cause to the cause with the highest priority.
608:         // 1: An ebreak instruction was executed. (priority 3)
609:         // 2: The Trigger Module caused a breakpoint exception. (priority 4)
610:         // 3: The debugger requested entry to Debug Mode. (priority 2)
611:         // 4: The hart single stepped because step was set. (priority 1)
612:         // we are currently not in debug mode and could potentially enter
613:         if (!debug_mode_q) begin
614:             dcsr_d.prv = priv_lvl_o;
615:             // trigger module fired
616: 
617:             // caused by a breakpoint
618:             if (ex_i.valid && ex_i.cause == riscv::BREAKPOINT) begin
619:                 // check that we actually want to enter debug depending on the privilege level we are currently in
620:                 unique case (priv_lvl_o)
621:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_M: begin
622:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
623:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
624:                     end
625:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_S: begin
626:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
627:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
628:                     end

631:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaku;
632:                     end
633:                     default:;
634:                 endcase
635:                 // save PC of next this instruction e.g.: the next one to be executed

637:                 dcsr_d.cause = dm::CauseBreakpoint;
638:             end
639:

645:                 // enter debug mode

647:                 // jump to the base address

## OBLI-001: Debug Module AXI Master — No Address Firewall

**Evidence from RTL:**

`fpga/src/ariane_xilinx.sv` lines 191-212 show the AXI crossbar with address ranges for all slaves (Debug, ROM, CLINT, PLIC, UART, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM). Line 269 connects the debug AXI master to `slave[1]` of this crossbar: `axi_master_connect i_axi_master_dm (.axi_req_i(dm_axi_m_req), .axi_resp_o(dm_axi_m_resp), .master(slave[1]));`. Line 258 shows `dm_top` producing `axi_m_req_o` from the System Bus Access (SBA) module (`dm_sba i_dm_sba` at `src/debug/dm_top.sv` line 150). No firewall IP or address-range filter sits between the debug master and any slave.

In `openpiton/serpent_peripherals.sv` line 196: `assign dm_axi_m_resp = '0;` — the debug master responses are tied off, meaning SBA is disabled in OpenPiton builds.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **subject** | Debug module AXI master (`dm_top` → `dm_sba` → `axi_m_req_o`) |
| **operation** | Read / Write |
| **object** | All memory-mapped slaves: ROM, CLINT, PLIC, UART, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM |
| **expected_guard** | Address-range filter, firewall, or privilege-de-escalation logic |
| **observed_behavior** | In FPGA configuration, debug master connects directly to AXI crossbar with full access to all address ranges; no firewall present. In OpenPiton, SBA responses are tied to 0 (disabled). |
| **path** | dmi_jtag → dm_top(SBA) → axi_master_connect → slave[1] → axi_xbar → all peripheral masters |
| **state_condition** | dmactive (from dmcontrol_q.dmactive via DMI write), sbcs_q controls SBA access |
| **impact** | A debugger can read/write any memory-mapped asset including boot ROM, CLINT, PLIC, peripheral registers, and DRAM, bypassing all CPU PMP and privilege checks. |
| **rtl_evidence** | `fpga/src/ariane_xilinx.sv:269`, `fpga/src/ariane_xilinx.sv:191-212`, `src/debug/dm_top.sv:150-208`, `openpiton/serpent_peripherals.sv:196` |
| **candidate_violation** | ✅ (for FPGA configuration; mitigated in OpenPiton) |

**Evidence from RTL:**

`src/csr_regfile.sv` line 938: `assign priv_lvl_o = (debug_mode_q || umode_i) ? riscv::PRIV_LVL_M : priv_lvl_q;` — when halted in debug mode, effective privilege is **always M-mode**, regardless of the privilege level at which the hart was halted.

`src/csr_regfile.sv` lines 852-863: CSR privilege check compares `priv_lvl_o` against `csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl`. In debug mode, `priv_lvl_o = M`, so all CSRs pass the privilege check. Debug-only CSRs (0x7Bx) require `debug_mode_q` (line 859).

`src/debug/dm_csrs.sv` lines 334-344: Abstract commands are issued via DMI write to the Command register, which sets `cmd_valid_o` and populates `command_q`. The abstract command then executes via the program buffer or abstract register access.
